assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response explains spot instance interruptions and provides commands to monitor terminating instances, acknowledging the preemption risk during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly mention that workloads on spot pools are typically designed to handle interruption, making them more suitable for upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends smaller surge settings (max-surge=1) for spot pools, which contradicts this expectation

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response shows max-unavailable=0, which contradicts this expectation of setting it greater than 0

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking spot instance availability and notes that spot pricing and interruption rates affect the process

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response provides a specific PodDisruptionBudget YAML example and emphasizes configuring appropriate disruption budgets

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response includes the specific gcloud container node-pools update command with surge settings parameters

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response recommends the opposite - upgrading on-demand nodes first for cluster stability, then spot instances
